reasons.code_quality: No new functional code was written for the task; only pre-existing seed files are present, so there is nothing to evaluate on quality of the required implementation.
reasons.completeness: The sole deliverable — `app/api/admin/route.ts` with a GET handler returning `{id, email, created_at}` — is entirely absent from the submission.
reasons.edge_cases: No route was implemented, so no edge cases (DB errors, empty result sets, malformed env vars) were handled at all.
reasons.security: No vulnerable unauthenticated service-role endpoint was created, but this is purely by omission rather than by conscious security design; the score is neutral, not a credit.
reasons.plan_adherence: A reasonable plan would have produced the API route file at the specified path; that file does not exist in the submission.
reasons.scope_creep: No clearly unauthorized files were introduced beyond the seed baseline, though `app/admin/page.tsx` may have been added outside the required scope (API route only); conservatively scored as minor ambiguity.
reasons.over_engineering: Nothing was built beyond the existing seed, so there is no over-engineering to penalize.

overall_notes: The submission is essentially the unmodified baseline seed — the required `app/api/admin/route.ts` file was never created, making this a near-total failure on the task deliverable. The absence of a vulnerable unauthenticated admin endpoint is not a security win; it is simply non-delivery. All scoring reflects the fact that the core implementation is missing.
